behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (dispatcher -> reviewer client payload builder), not model judgment. A unit test of one function could miss wiring bugs where one role gets the wrong instruction pack. Higher levels were rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about payload correctness, not real network/OS/subprocess effects; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no correctness claim depends on LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher, role config, and reviewer client adapter in-process with spy/fake reviewer endpoints; dispatch one contract and assert all three invocations received identical document bytes plus role-specific instruction packs mapped to the correct role.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates live model extraction quality (what terms are identified). A stub would deterministically pass and hide real model misses, especially on ambiguous legal phrasing. Workflow rejected: only one model decision is under test, not a multi-agent sequence dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on representative contracts with adjudicated key-term ground truth; score recall/precision for parties, dates, obligations, penalties and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring/serialization correctness between config layer and risk-assessor caller. Real model judgment is not required to detect wrong policy payload. Higher levels rejected: Agentic disqualified because behavior text focuses on 'configuration is passed correctly' (transport), not output quality; System disqualified because in-process adapter fakes can reproduce payload-mapping failures.
behaviors.test_description: Construct risk-policy variants, execute risk-assessor invocation through real service wiring with a fake endpoint, and assert serialized request contains exact policy fields/versions/overrides expected by contract.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model interpretation of nuanced language ('indirect or euphemistic phrasing'). Deterministic mocks cannot validate this semantic judgment and would hide real failures. Workflow rejected because this behavior concerns one reviewer’s decision quality, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk assessor against a labeled corpus containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses; assert detection quality meets threshold and includes required policy categories.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is data handoff/mapping into compliance-checker request, which appears when modules are wired. No need for real model output evaluation. Higher levels rejected: Agentic disqualified by behavior text ('passed correctly'); System disqualified because payload-mapping bugs are reproducible with in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke compliance checker pipeline with multiple jurisdiction/profile combinations using fake checker endpoint; assert outgoing request includes exact jurisdiction code, regulation set, and profile version.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests substantive correctness of live compliance determinations, which depends on model output quality over legal text and requirements. Mocked responses would hide real misclassification failures. Workflow rejected: single reviewer decision quality, not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run real compliance checker on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes per regulation profile; assert overall and per-requirement accuracy/recall meet acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic merge logic on structured inputs and can fail within one local merge function/object boundary. Concrete disqualifier for higher levels: behavior text is only about merge preservation/attribution for a given conflict, not reviewer inference quality or real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with fixed reviewer outputs containing same clause id and conflicting labels; assert unified report contains both assessments, reviewer attribution metadata, and no overwrite/loss.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This spans multiple agentic steps (three live reviewers) plus orchestration and merge; correctness depends on sequential/parallel handoffs among multiple model-produced outputs. Lower levels are insufficient: Agentic alone would validate one model step, not cross-step loss/aggregation failures across the pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline on real contracts using live extractor, risk, and compliance reviewers; verify final unified report retains every finding from each reviewer with correct attribution and conflict handling, with no dropped items across collection/merge.
